content.text:
Looking at the image, the track runs straight ahead with the car positioned on the left edge of the asphalt, and the road extends clearly to the right — the car needs to continue correcting rightward toward center.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 118, target = (118 + 5) % 121 = 123 % 121 = 2
- Target WP index 2: x=3.3593, y=0.6834
- Car: x=3.0168, y=0.9150, heading=-7.177°
- dy = 0.6834 - 0.9150 = -0.2316
- dx = 3.3593 - 3.0168 = 0.3425
- bearing = atan2(-0.2316, 0.3425) = -34.07°
- delta = -34.07° - (-7.177°) = -26.89°
- Waypoint is to the RIGHT → negative steering

Delta = -26.89°, still significant right correction needed. Car is 0.232m left of center (track width 0.762m). Continue strong right steering.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Car is still 0.232m left of center on a straight track with delta=-26.9° to target waypoint, requiring continued strong right steering to return to centerline.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn (CCW), negative = right turn (CW)",
    "Track centerline y~0.683 on this straight section; car y~0.915 means car is far left of center",
    "Car has been persistently left of center despite -25° steering — very slow correction",
    "When delta > 25°, use speed=0.75 and steering=-25 for maximum correction",
    "Track runs nearly due east for next ~20 waypoints (y stays ~0.68)",
    "Once delta < 5°, switch to 0 steering to avoid oscillation",
    "Track width 0.76m — very narrow, precision required",
    "Progress 99.75% — nearly complete lap, stay on track at all costs",
    "All wheels on track — car is on asphalt but left of center",
    "Heading ~-7° but need bearing ~-34° — large negative delta persists"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [2.928, 2.970, 3.017],
    "y": [0.958, 0.941, 0.915],
    "yaw": [-0.04, -3.34, -7.18]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP2 at (3.359, 0.683): dy=-0.232, dx=0.343, bearing=-34.07°, delta=-26.89° → strong right correction needed",
  "speed": 0.75,
  "steering_angle": -25.0
}
```
